title: Blue Origin Rocket Blows Up on Florida Launchpad During Test
description: The rocket, built by the Jeff Bezos-owned space company, was to carry 48 satellites into space. Blue Origin reported on social media that “all personnel have been accounted for.”
author: Kenneth Chang

title: Israel to Cut Ties With U.N. Chief Over Its Inclusion on Sexual Violence Blacklist
description: The United Nations secretary general, António Guterres, told Israel there had been an “increasing number of cases” of sexual violence against Palestinian detainees, according to a letter shared by Israel’s U.N. ambassador.
author: Ephrat Livni

title: Judge Declines, for Now, to Block Mail-In Voting Changes Ordered by Trump
description: The ruling allowed the Trump administration to continue to pursue changes that include enlisting the Postal Service in checking voters against a national database.
author: Zach Montague and Adam Sella

title: Scooter Rider and Cyclist Are Killed in Head-On Crash on N.Y.C. Bridge
description: The collision on the Queensboro Bridge involved an e-scooter that is not legally allowed to be used on the streets of New York.
author: Stefanos Chen and Claire Fahy

title: Bari Weiss Names Nick Bilton to Lead CBS’s ‘60 Minutes’ in Major Shake-Up
description: Bari Weiss, CBS’s editor in chief, named Nick Bilton, a tech journalist and filmmaker, as the show’s executive producer. The network also fired two on-air correspondents.
author: Benjamin Mullin and Michael M. Grynbaum

title: Justice Dept. Is Said to Open Criminal Inquiry of E. Jean Carroll, Who Accused Trump of Rape
description: Ms. Carroll, who prevailed in a civil trial after accusing President Trump of sexual abuse, is the latest target in a Justice Department campaign going after his perceived enemies.
author: Glenn Thrush and Benjamin Weiser

title: Israel’s Isolation Is Deepening Fast. It Doesn’t Have to Be This Way.
description: Elections this year hold out the promise that the state can change policies in a way to recover some international good will.
author: Shira Efron

title: Dell Gets a $9.7 Billion Defense Contract. Trump’s Portfolio Stands to Benefit.
description: The president’s family says his stock purchases are automatically timed, but recent acquisitions of the computer company’s shares raise questions.
author: Eric Lipton

title: He’s Entitled and Nepotistic. This Is Not What Democrats Need.
description: Members of the country’s grandest political dynasty are not inherently qualified to hold office.
author: Michelle Cottle

title: Three Ways Trump Is Losing the War in Iran
description: Three reasons Trump is losing the war in Iran.
author: David French

title: The Mideast Is Baffled by Trump’s Call to Expand Abraham Accords
description: The president said more countries should be required to recognize Israel as part of a deal to end the war with Iran. Analysts say the chances of that happening are close to zero.
author: Vivian Nereim, Isabel Kershner and Elian Peltier

title: What Cubans Really Think About Trump
description: A filmmaker asks residents of the embattled island what they would say to the U.S. president.
author: Jeff Seal

title: Paul McCartney on His New Album and the Beatles’ Earliest Days
description: On his new album, the ex-Beatle revisits his past and revels in simply continuing to create.
author: Jon Pareles

title: Not Every Language Has Spelling Bees. Here’s Why English Is Perfect for Them.
description: English’s hybrid history, silent letters and overcomplicated rules are what make the competition fierce.
author: Nitsuh Abebe

title: The Battle Over Trump’s Immigration Crackdown Comes to a Newark ICE Facility Parking Lot
description: For five days, activists have gathered outside a detention center in New Jersey to denounce conditions inside. Federal officials have rejected those concerns.
author: Ana Ley and Mark Bonamo

title: F.B.I. Arrests C.I.A. Official With $40 Million in Gold Bars in His Home
description: The only charge lodged against David Rush is that he inflated his academic credentials and obtained military leave pay worth tens of thousands of dollars.
author: Devlin Barrett and Julian E. Barnes

title: Why the Ebola and Hantavirus Outbreaks Have Confounded Scientists
description: The types of Ebola and hantavirus worrying officials are very different from the species identified decades ago, raising new questions about how to respond.
author: Carl Zimmer

title: It’s Crow, Mr. Trump, Not Lobster
description: Whatever deal is reached between the United States and Iran, Trump will have to make a big concession: A murderous regime remains intact.
author: Thomas L. Friedman

title: Writing Is Fundamental to How We Think
description: A.I. can be a crutch that hurts our ability to think creatively.
author: Rebecca Winthrop

title: Reflecting Pool Contract Has ‘Inflated’ Profit Margin, Government Analysis Finds
description: The firm that was given a no-bid contract to fix the troubled landmark is charging 20 percent. The typical profit margin is 6 percent to 12 percent, internal records show.
author: David A. Fahrenthold
